Title: When the live preview says unsupported
Summary: Catalog of every translator rejection reason, why each one rejects, and what to do about it. The editor keeps working when the preview can't run.
Body:
## What the banner means

When the translator's parser rejects your source, a red banner replaces the canvas with **"Live preview unsupported"** + a reason + a line number. The editor itself stays fully usable — you can keep typing, save, view usages, everything except the live preview.

The failure modes:

## shader_type isn't canvas_item

> warn: shader_type "spatial" isn't in the v1 translator subset. Only canvas_item is supported for live preview.

**Why**: spatial / particles / sky / fog need different vertex stages and different built-in sets (`ALBEDO`, `NORMAL`, `ROUGHNESS` for spatial; `VELOCITY`, `LIFETIME` for particles; etc.). Running them on the canvas_item full-screen quad would just produce wrong output, so we fail loudly.

**What to do**: author in Bleepforge if you like (everything except the preview works), but render in Godot to see it. The translator may grow other types in a later phase.

## varying declarations

> warn: varying declarations are out of v1 scope — we only run the fragment stage on a full-screen quad.

**Why**: `varying` declares data passed from the vertex stage to the fragment stage. Bleepforge runs only the fragment stage on a fixed full-screen quad, so the vertex side that would populate the varying doesn't exist.

**What to do**: move whatever data the `varying` carried into a `uniform` instead, then drive it from the [auto-generated uniform controls](/help/shaders/uniform-controls). If the data is per-vertex (literally varies across the quad), the live preview can't reproduce it — render in Godot.

## hint_depth_texture / hint_normal_roughness_texture

> warn: hint_depth_texture requires a depth buffer from a 3D scene, which canvas_item shaders running on the preview canvas don't have.

**Why**: these hints request 3D-scene buffers Bleepforge's 2D preview can't produce. `hint_depth_texture` needs a depth buffer (only meaningful for 3D `spatial` shaders); `hint_normal_roughness_texture` needs a deferred-rendering normal/roughness G-buffer.

**What to do**: render in Godot — these aren't meaningful for canvas_item anyway. Bleepforge's `hint_screen_texture` IS supported (see [translator subset](/help/shaders/translator-subset)) and aliases to the picked test image, so post-process viewport shaders preview cleanly.

## Uniform name collides with an injected symbol

> warn: uniform name "u_time" collides with a translator-injected symbol (built-in, varying, or prelude uniform). Rename the uniform to something Bleepforge doesn't already emit.

**Why**: the translator emits a fixed prelude containing names like `u_time`, `u_texture`, `u_resolution`, `v_uv`, `fragColor`, plus local-built-ins like `TIME`, `UV`, `COLOR`, `MODULATE`. A user uniform with one of these names would produce a duplicate declaration in the emitted GLSL, and WebGL's error ("redeclaration of u_time") wouldn't point at the right fix.

**What to do**: rename the offending uniform. Anything that doesn't appear in the [translator subset reference](/help/shaders/translator-subset)'s prelude / built-ins list is fair game.

## #include directives

> warn: #include directives are out of v1 scope. Inline the helper code or wait for translator Phase 4.

**Why**: `#include "helpers.gdshaderinc"` would need to resolve relative paths, fetch the file, splice the contents into the source before parsing — straightforward but not done in v1.

**What to do**: inline the included code for previewing, then split back into includes when shipping. Both sources can coexist; just keep them in sync. Or wait for Phase 4 — `.gdshaderinc` support is on the deferred list.

## Missing shader_type

> warn: No shader_type declaration found. The translator needs a `shader_type canvas_item;` line to know what stage to compile.

**Why**: without `shader_type`, we don't know what built-ins to expose. Godot would error on this too — it's not a Bleepforge-specific restriction.

**What to do**: add `shader_type canvas_item;` as the first non-comment line.

## WebGL compile errors (separate from translator rejection)

The translator can accept source that WebGL then rejects — e.g. you reference an undeclared variable, or call a function that doesn't exist in GLSL ES 3.00. Those show up in the same banner with line-anchored entries:

- `line 12: 'foo' : undeclared identifier`
- `line 15: 'textureLod' : function not supported in this version`

Lines map back to your source via the emitter's source-map fields. Fix the GLSL-level issue and the preview comes back live.

## What stays working when the preview is dark

Everything else: CodeMirror editing, syntax highlighting, save, Ctrl+S, dirty indicator, delete, duplicate, the usages panel, the external-change banner. Only the canvas + the auto-generated uniform controls go quiet.
